title: In situ sequencing for RNA analysis in tissue sections
author: Chika Yokota
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bb2giqbw
materials: Fresh frozen tissue samples
OCT compound
DEPC-PBS
0.1 M HCl
Ethanol (EtOH)
SecureSeal hybridization chambers
DEPC-PBS-T
TranscriptMe Buffer
formamide
Reagents for reverse transcription were combined as in the table below. Note: All following tables in this protocol have been calculated to a final volume of 50 µl and should be adjusted accordingly.

%s %s Reagents Stock [Final] 1x (µl) 200 DEPC H20 34.75 139 TranscriptMe Buffer 10x 1x 5 20 dNTPs 25 mM 500 µM 1 4 BSA 20 µg/µl 0.2 mg/ml 0.5 2 Random Decamers 100 µM 5 µM 2.5 10 Riblock 40 U/µl 1 U/ml 1.25 5 RT Transcript Me 200 U/µl 20 U/ml 5 20 Total 50 200

DEPC-PBS removed and combined reagents added to seal chamber. Chamber is sealed and slide placed in a humidity chamber. Slide and chamber is incubated from 6 hours to overnight at 37°C. 37 °C, Incubator
Reverse transcription reagents are removed and postfixation performed with 3% (w/v) paraformaldehyde in DEPC-PBS for 30 min at RT. 00:30:00, PFA fixation
Reagents for padlock hybridization are combined as in the table below.

DEPC-PBS-T removed and combined reagents added to seal chamber. Chamber is sealed and placed in a humidity chamber. Slide and chamber is incubated for 30 min at 37°C. Enzymes and temperature (see Note 12 ) 37 °C 00:30:00 Then switched to incubation at 45°C for 1 hour. 45 °C 01:00:00
Reagents for rolling circle amplification (RCA) of padlock probes are combined as in the table below.

DEPC-PBS-T removed and combined reagents added to seal chamber. Chamber is sealed and incubated in humidity chamber for 4 hours at 37°C OR 30°C overnight. (see Note 13 ) 03:00:00, 37C incubation 37 °C OR 30 °C
Reagents for anchor primer hybridization are combined according to table below.
Reagents added to chamber and incubated 30 min at RT, in the dark. 00:30:00, Anchor Incubation
Reagents for base library hybridization and ligation are combined according to table below.
DEPC-PBS-T removed and combined reagents added to seal chamber. Slide is incubated 1 hour at RT in the dark. 01:00:00, SBL
Combine reagents in the table below for the stripping of anchor and sequence primer.
Reagents added to section and incubated at RT for 30 min. 00:30:00
Sections rinsed with DEPC-PBS two times (x2).
%s %s Safety precautions: formamide
Then the slide is washed twice with DEPC-PBS-T.
%s %s
Slide is washed twice with DEPC-PBS-T.
Rehydrate with DEPC-PBS-T.
Sections washed once with DEPC-PBS-T

steps.title: Fresh frozen tissue samples embedded in...
steps.instruction: Fresh frozen tissue samples embedded in OCT compound and stored at -80°C. Tissue is cryosectioned at 10 μm and collected on Superfrost Slides and can be stored at -80°C until fixation and start of procedure. (see Note 9 )

steps.title: Slides are taken from -80°C and...
steps.instruction: Slides are taken from -80°C and left at room temperature (RT) for 3 min to air dry. 00:03:00, Thawing Fixation performed with freshly prepared 3% (w/v) paraformaldehyde in DEPC-PBS for 5 min at RT. PFA solution is applied directly on top of the section. (see Note 10 ) Safety precautions: paraformaldehyde 00:05:00, Fixation

steps.title: The tissue is permeabilized with 0
steps.instruction: The tissue is permeabilized with 0.1 M HCl (in H 2 O) at RT for 5 min. Glass slide is dipped in a ~20 ml solution of 0.1 M HCl. Safety precautions: HCl 00:05:00, HCl treatment

steps.title: After permeabilization, slides are washed in...
steps.instruction: After permeabilization, slides are washed in DEPC-PBS for 1 min at RT. 00:01:00, PBS Wash Rinse slide 2x more times with DEPC-PBS

steps.title: Slides are dehydrated in an ethanol (EtOH) series
steps.instruction: Slides are dehydrated in an ethanol (EtOH) series. 70% EtOH, RT, 1 min 00:01:00, 70% EtOH 100% EtOH, RT, 1 min 00:01:00, 100% EtOH

steps.title: Slide sections are air dried
steps.instruction: Slide sections are air dried. When dry, SecureSeal hybridization chambers mounted over tissue. Use a chamber size that covers your desired tissue.

steps.title: Sections are rehydrated with DEPC-PBS-T and then once with DEPC-PBS
steps.instruction: Sections are rehydrated with DEPC-PBS-T and then once with DEPC-PBS. (see Note 11 )

steps.title: Wash twice with DEPC-PBS-T
steps.instruction: Wash twice with DEPC-PBS-T.

steps.title: After RCA, samples washed twice with DEPC-PBS-T
steps.instruction: After RCA, samples washed twice with DEPC-PBS-T. Samples protected from light.

steps.title: Dip slides into 70% EtOH solution...
steps.instruction: Dip slides into 70% EtOH solution and incubate for 1 min. 00:01:00, 70% EtOH

steps.title: Remove the SecureSeal chamber and dip...
steps.instruction: Remove the SecureSeal chamber and dip slides in 100% for 1 min (see Note 14 ) 00:01:00, 100% EtOH Air dry the slides.

steps.title: Apply small amount (~10 µL) of...
steps.instruction: Apply small amount (~10 µL) of mounting medium (Slowfade or similar) and apply a cover slip. (see Note 15 ) Slide can be stored in the dark and at +4°C for longer term storage before imaging.

steps.title: Use a standard epifluorescent microscope with 20x objective
steps.instruction: Use a standard epifluorescent microscope with 20x objective. Use DAPI staining to focus on section as a reference point to visualize RCA products. For details of microscope setup, check referenced publications.

steps.title: Acquire images as needed using reference...
steps.instruction: Acquire images as needed using reference points that can be used for sequential imaging of other bases later on. RCPs are located in different focal planes, therefore, a series of images should be captured at different focal depths. The stacks of images are then merged to a single image using the maximum-intensity projection.

steps.title: After every round of base imaging,...
steps.instruction: After every round of base imaging, fluorophores and probes need to be stripped of the rolling circle products in order to hybridize the next base. Remove coverslip by dipping slide into 70% EtOH until it slips off. Note: After second and all subsequent rounds of imaging, cover slip can be removed by dipping slide into PBS, dehydration step is not needed if hydrophobic pen is used.

steps.title: Dip slide into 100% EtOH for...
steps.instruction: Dip slide into 100% EtOH for 1 min and then air dry the samples. 00:01:00, 100% EtOH

steps.title: Use a hydrophobic pen to draw a barrier around section
steps.instruction: Use a hydrophobic pen to draw a barrier around section. Alternatively, mount another SecureSeal chamber.

steps.title: Sections washed with 100% formamide at RT for 3 min
steps.instruction: Sections washed with 100% formamide at RT for 3 min. 00:03:00, 1st Wash Repeat an additional two times for a total of 3 times. 00:03:00, 2nd Wash 00:03:00, 3rd Wash

steps.title: Repeat Steps 22-44 for additional required bases. Note
steps.instruction: Repeat Steps 22-44 for additional required bases. Note: If using hydrophobic pen, chamber will not be used and solutions can just be applied on top of section during incubations.

steps.title: For most up to date methodolgy...
steps.instruction: For most up to date methodolgy on image analysis, we refer to a recent publication from our group: Probabilistic cell typing enables fine mapping of closely related cell types in situ Qian X, Harris KD, Hauling T, Nicoloutsopoulos D, Muñoz-Manchado AB, Skene N, Hjerling-Leffler J, Nilsson M Nat Methods. 2020 Jan;17(1):101-106. doi: 10.1038/s41592-019-0631-4 We also refer to the publication this protocol is based on for image analysis: In situ sequencing for RNA analysis in preserved tissue and cells. Ke R, Mignardi M, Pacureanu A, Svedlund J, Botling J, Wählby C, Nilsson M. Nat Methods. 2013 Sep;10(9):857-60. doi: 10.1038/nmeth.2563 (Cell profiler: see Note16 )
